mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending forecasts, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive when both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously in the same month.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a declining industrial production index reflects supply-side constraints that compress corporate profit margins, generating negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 20-day forward returns during periods of rising federal funds rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 285, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing markets to price in aggressive policy tightening that produces negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 285.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and corporate revenue projections, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in a majority of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where quarterly GDP growth accelerates while monthly retail sales decelerate signals inventory accumulation and weak final demand, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive when GDPC1 growth outpaces RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 12-month moving average, persistent supply chain bottlenecks constrain output capacity and trigger negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of periods where INDPRO trades below its 12-month moving average.
